title: Diasperus berteroanus
intro: Diasperus berteroanus, commonly known as Puna root or Puerto Rican Puna root, is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Diasperus berteroanus include alkaloids, such as diasporeine and berteroin, as well as lignans and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Diasperus berteroanus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including bronchitis and skin infections.
